diagrams.title: Inheritance Hierarchy for Shapes
diagrams.description: Shows a classic inheritance hierarchy with an abstract base class 'Shape' and two concrete derived classes 'Circle' and 'Rectangle'. This demonstrates the 'is-a' relationship where both Circle and Rectangle are types of Shape, inheriting common properties like color and position along with pure virtual function draw().
elements: Abstract base class 'Shape' box with <<abstract>> stereotype
Shared attributes 'color' and 'position' in base class
diagrams.purpose: Explains how inheritance establishes an 'is-a' relationship and how abstract classes with pure virtual functions define interfaces that derived classes must implement

diagrams.title: Polymorphic Behavior Through Virtual Functions
diagrams.description: Demonstrates runtime polymorphism by showing how a base class pointer (Shape*) can call the appropriate derived class method at runtime. The diagram shows three scenarios: a Circle object, a Rectangle object, and a Triangle object all being drawn through the same base pointer, producing different outputs.
elements: Three object instances: Circle, Rectangle, Triangle
Dotted lines showing which method gets called
diagrams.purpose: Illustrates how virtual functions enable runtime polymorphism, where the same function call produces different behaviors based on the actual object type at runtime

diagrams.title: Override Keyword and Access Specifiers
diagrams.description: Shows the inheritance access control in C++ with public, protected, and private inheritance. Also demonstrates the 'override' keyword usage to ensure derived class methods correctly override base class virtual functions, preventing subtle bugs.
elements: Three inheritance types: public, protected, private
Base class members with different access levels
Derived class showing inherited member visibility
Error indication when override is missing or incorrect
diagrams.purpose: Explains C++ inheritance access control rules and the importance of the override keyword for safe polymorphic code
